lines: The chain hangs above the road. Susanna’s certified abstract is dry enough to fold.
Beyond the last grave plots, Old Pilgrim Way narrows between dead fields and the first Hallowfen markers.

questUpdate.log: You leave South Measure by the opened north road.

showBriefing.title: OLD PILGRIM WAY
pages: South Measure recedes behind its raised chain. Water strikes the settling tanks in an even rhythm, or in the measured pauses of rationing.
The signed abstract in your pack names who controls that rhythm. It does not make the decision harmless.

page: Compact clerks open a new pump register before you reach the last grave. Patients watch their water improve beneath numbers they did not write.
A Morrow service cage stands beside the court. Fresh filters arrive with a debt column left blank for now.
Noa’s slate remains above the resident key table. Tobit is already arguing with the first maintenance interval.
The undercroft stays shut. Every cup is measured in public while the old intake keeps its answer below.
Brother Cassian’s tread assembly takes the east shoulder. His damaged vox counts nineteen pulses, then waits through six measures of silence.
Cassian’s tread marks are already ahead of you. At each culvert he has cut one small warning into the road stone.
Cassian turns west with the intake record sealed against his breastplate. The Black Reliquary will hear the impossible reply in his report.
Cassian remains beside the water court. His weapon mount faces the pipe, not the households waiting at the taps.
Behind you, someone begins Salome’s second lesson in a voice too low to carry to the road. Three adults know where the west route opens.

lines: The north chain is still drawn across Old Pilgrim Way.
Susanna will not raise it until the pump, the household roll, Noa, and Cassian have answers the settlement can hear.
